title: PESTO campaign scheduling UI: banner toggle vs. dropdown
summary: Does a scheduling dropdown instead of a banner toggle increase PESTO adoption? Yes, it did.
hypothesis: If we move PESTO into the scheduling flow as a dropdown option rather than a persistent banner toggle, users will discover and enable it more intentionally, leading to increased adoption and campaign scheduling rates.
results: The dropdown treatment increased PESTO adoption by 19% (p < 0.01). Campaign scheduling rates also improved. The experiment was called at 93% power after approximately 3 weeks.
learning: Placing PESTO as a contextual option within the scheduling flow drives more intentional discovery and adoption compared to a persistent banner toggle.
notes: Design screenshots in Slack thread. Control: pink PESTO banner toggle. Treatment: scheduling dropdown with PESTO option.
